name: moon_build_wave_c_shared_controller
outcome_goal: Extract shared build controller from BuilderPage and BuildWorkspace. Create event-driven React hook that subscribes to the new SSE endpoint instead of polling.

jobs.label: build_controller_types
jobs.prompt:
TASK: Create canonical TypeScript types for the build system, extracted from inline definitions in BuilderPage and BuildWorkspace.

CONTEXT:
- BuildWorkspace.tsx (~1500 lines) in `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/build/` defines BuildPayload, BuildNode, BuildEdge, BuildIssue, CompiledSpecProjection inline.
- BuilderPage.tsx (~1200 lines) in `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/` defines PipelineModel, PipelineStep types.
- Build state events from the new SSE endpoint deliver: workflow_id, event_type, node_id, subpath, payload_json.

WHAT TO DO:

Create `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/shared/types.ts` with:

1. All build-related types currently defined inline in BuildWorkspace.tsx:
   - BuildPayload (workflow, definition, compiled_spec, planning_notes, build_state, build_blockers, build_graph, binding_ledger, import_snapshots, authority_attachments, build_issues, projection_status, compiled_spec_projection)
   - BuildNode (node_id, kind, title, summary, route, prompt, required_inputs, outputs, source_node_ids, binding_ids, issue_ids, status, gate_rule)
   - BuildEdge (edge_id, source_node_id, target_node_id, edge_type, label)
   - BuildIssue (issue_id, severity, message, node_ids, suggestion, kind)
   - CompiledSpecProjection (jobs, triggers, status fields)
   - BindingLedgerEntry, ImportSnapshot, AuthorityAttachment

2. New event types:
   - BuildEvent { id: number, workflow_id: string, event_type: string, node_id: string, subpath: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>, created_at: string }
   - BuildEventType = 'mutation' | 'compilation' | 'commit' | 'refinement' | 'planning'

3. Shared controller types:
   - BuildController { loadWorkflow(id: string): Promise<BuildPayload>, saveWorkflow(id: string, definition: unknown): Promise<void>, compileDefinition(prose: string): Promise<BuildPayload>, commitDefinition(id: string): Promise<BuildPayload>, postBuildMutation(id: string, subpath: string, body: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<BuildPayload> }

RULES:
- Export everything. Other modules will import from this file.
- Use TypeScript interfaces, not types (for extensibility).
- Match the actual JSON shape returned by the REST API. Read BuildWorkspace.tsx to get the exact shapes.
- No runtime code, just type definitions.

OUTPUT: New: shared/types.ts

jobs.label: extract_build_controller
jobs.prompt:
TASK: Extract shared build data loading and mutation functions from BuilderPage and BuildWorkspace into a shared controller module.

CONTEXT:
- BuilderPage.tsx has: loadWorkflow (fetch /api/workflows/{id}), saveWorkflow (POST /api/workflows/{id}), file upload (POST /api/files).
- BuildWorkspace.tsx has: loadWorkflow (fetch /api/workflows/{id}/build), compileDefinition (POST /api/compile), refineDefinition (POST /api/refine-definition), commitDefinition (POST /api/commit), postBuildMutation (POST /api/workflows/{id}/build/{suffix} — used for 6+ mutation types).
- Both components handle errors with try/catch and state updates.

WHAT TO DO:

Create `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/shared/buildController.ts` with:

1. `loadWorkflowDefinition(workflowId: string): Promise<any>` — fetches /api/workflows/{id}
2. `loadWorkflowBuild(workflowId: string): Promise<BuildPayload>` — fetches /api/workflows/{id}/build
3. `saveWorkflowDefinition(workflowId: string, definition: any): Promise<any>` — POST /api/workflows/{id}
4. `compileDefinition(prose: string, title?: string): Promise<BuildPayload>` — POST /api/compile
5. `refineDefinition(prose: string, definition: any): Promise<BuildPayload>` — POST /api/refine-definition
6. `commitDefinition(workflowId: string): Promise<BuildPayload>` — POST /api/commit
7. `postBuildMutation(workflowId: string, subpath: string, body: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<BuildPayload>` — POST /api/workflows/{id}/build/{subpath}

Each function:
- Makes the fetch call with proper headers (Content-Type: application/json)
- Parses the JSON response
- Throws on non-ok responses with the error message from the body
- Returns the typed result

RULES:
- Pure functions, no React dependencies. Just async fetch wrappers.
- Import types from shared/types.ts.
- Don't modify BuilderPage or BuildWorkspace yet — that's a separate job.

OUTPUT: New: shared/buildController.ts

jobs.label: build_events_hook
wire_shared_controller
jobs.prompt:
TASK: Rewire BuilderPage and BuildWorkspace to use the shared controller and types instead of inline definitions.

CONTEXT:
- shared/types.ts now has all build-related TypeScript types.
- shared/buildController.ts now has all API call functions.
- shared/hooks/useBuildEvents.ts and useBuildPayload.ts now provide event-driven state.
- BuilderPage.tsx currently defines its own fetch calls and types inline.
- BuildWorkspace.tsx currently defines its own fetch calls, types, and mutation handlers inline.

WHAT TO DO:

1. In BuildWorkspace.tsx:
   - Replace inline type definitions with imports from shared/types.ts
   - Replace inline fetch calls (loadWorkflow, compile, refine, commit, postBuildMutation) with imports from shared/buildController.ts
   - Replace the manual polling/reload pattern with useBuildPayload hook where possible (or at minimum import the individual functions)
   - Keep all JSX, state management, and UI logic unchanged

2. In BuilderPage.tsx:
   - Replace inline fetch calls (loadWorkflow, saveWorkflow) with imports from shared/buildController.ts
   - Keep pipeline editing, step management, and all other logic unchanged

3. Verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` from the surfaces/app directory to check for type errors.

RULES:
- This is a REFACTOR. Zero behavior change. Every user-visible behavior must remain identical.
- Don't rewrite the components. Just swap inline functions for imported ones.
- If a type doesn't exactly match, adjust the shared type to match (the component is the source of truth for the shape).
- If tsc fails, fix the type issues.

OUTPUT: Modified: BuilderPage.tsx, BuildWorkspace.tsx. Possibly adjusted: shared/types.ts
